title_en: The Book of Faith: Iman, Islam, and Ihsan
content_en:
The opening sections of Sharh al-Sunnah address the foundations of Islamic belief, beginning with the famous hadith of Jibril that defines the three dimensions of the religion: Islam (the outward acts of worship), Iman (faith and its articles), and Ihsan (the dimension of spiritual excellence). Al-Baghawi's treatment of this foundational hadith establishes the theological framework within which the rest of the work operates.

The Hadith of Jibril

Al-Baghawi presents the hadith of Jibril — in which the Angel Jibril appeared to the Prophet in the form of a man and asked him about Islam, Iman, Ihsan, and the signs of the Last Hour — as the master text for understanding the religion. The Prophet's definitions in this hadith: Islam as the five pillars (testimony, prayer, zakat, fasting, hajj); Iman as belief in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Last Day, and divine predetermination; and Ihsan as worshipping God as if you see Him, and if you do not see Him, knowing that He sees you — provide the organizing framework for al-Baghawi's entire work.

He explains the relationship between Islam and Iman, addressing the scholarly debate about whether they are identical or whether Iman is a subset of Islam or vice versa. The Murjite position (that Iman is purely internal belief, not including acts) and the Kharijite position (that sins exclude one from Iman entirely) are both rejected in favor of the mainstream Sunni view: Iman includes both belief and action, and increases and decreases with obedience and disobedience.

The Articles of Faith

Al-Baghawi presents the articles of Islamic faith systematically, drawing on the hadiths that establish each one. Belief in divine predetermination (qadar) receives particularly careful treatment, as it was the subject of the most bitter theological controversies of the early Islamic period. He explains the mainstream Sunni position: God has decreed all things and nothing occurs without His will and knowledge, but human beings have genuine choice and are responsible for their choices. This position charts a middle path between pure determinism (which would deny human moral responsibility) and the Mutazilite position (which al-Baghawi reads as limiting divine foreknowledge and power).

The Signs of the Last Hour

The final part of al-Baghawi's treatment of the hadith of Jibril addresses the signs of the Last Hour — the events that will precede the Day of Judgment. He presents the minor signs (already occurring in the Prophet's era and after) and the major signs (which will occur near the end of time). This section draws on a rich hadith tradition about eschatology that al-Baghawi presents with careful attention to the reliability of the individual hadiths and the various scholarly interpretations of their meaning.

His treatment of eschatology reflects the Sunni approach: affirm what is established by reliable texts, interpret metaphors as metaphors while taking the major events (the Dajjal, the descent of Isa, the emergence of Yajuj and Majuj, the rising of the sun from the west) as literal realities, and resist speculation about precise timing or sequence beyond what the texts establish. This interpretive conservatism is characteristic of al-Baghawi's broader methodological approach.
title_id: Kitab Iman: Iman, Islam, dan Ihsan
content_id:
Bagian-bagian pembuka dari Syarh al-Sunnah membahas fondasi-fondasi akidah Islam, dimulai dengan hadis Jibril yang terkenal yang mendefinisikan tiga dimensi agama: Islam (amalan-amalan ibadah lahiriah), Iman (keimanan dan rukun-rukunnya), dan Ihsan (dimensi keunggulan spiritual). Perlakuan al-Baghawi terhadap hadis fondasi ini menetapkan kerangka teologis di mana seluruh karya beroperasi.

Hadis Jibril

Al-Baghawi menyajikan hadis Jibril — di mana Malaikat Jibril muncul kepada Nabi ﷺ dalam wujud seorang pria dan menanyakannya tentang Islam, Iman, Ihsan, dan tanda-tanda Hari Akhir — sebagai teks utama untuk memahami agama. Definisi-definisi Nabi ﷺ dalam hadis ini: Islam sebagai lima pilar (syahadat, shalat, zakat, puasa, haji); Iman sebagai percaya kepada Allah, malaikat-malaikat-Nya, kitab-kitab-Nya, rasul-rasul-Nya, Hari Akhir, dan takdir ilahi; dan Ihsan sebagai menyembah Allah seolah-olah engkau melihat-Nya, dan jika engkau tidak melihat-Nya, maka sesungguhnya Ia melihatmu — memberikan kerangka pengorganisasian untuk seluruh karya al-Baghawi.

Ia menjelaskan hubungan antara Islam dan Iman, membahas perdebatan keilmuan tentang apakah keduanya identik atau apakah Iman adalah bagian dari Islam atau sebaliknya. Posisi Murji'ah (bahwa Iman adalah keyakinan internal semata, tidak termasuk amal) dan posisi Khawarij (bahwa dosa-dosa mengecualikan seseorang dari Iman sepenuhnya) keduanya ditolak demi pandangan Sunni arus utama: Iman mencakup keimanan maupun amal, dan bertambah serta berkurang dengan ketaatan dan kemaksiatan.

Rukun-Rukun Iman

Al-Baghawi menyajikan rukun-rukun iman Islam secara sistematis, mengandalkan hadis-hadis yang menetapkan masing-masing. Keyakinan terhadap takdir ilahi (qadar) mendapat perlakuan yang sangat cermat, karena menjadi subjek kontroversi teologis yang paling sengit dari periode Islam awal. Ia menjelaskan posisi Sunni arus utama: Allah telah menetapkan segalanya dan tidak ada yang terjadi tanpa kehendak dan pengetahuan-Nya, tetapi manusia memiliki pilihan yang nyata dan bertanggung jawab atas pilihan-pilihan mereka.

Tanda-Tanda Hari Akhir

Perlakuannya tentang eskatologi mencerminkan pendekatan Sunni: tegaskan apa yang ditetapkan oleh teks-teks yang andal, tafsirkan metafora sebagai metafora sambil menjadikan peristiwa-peristiwa besar (Dajjal, turunnya Isa, kemunculan Ya'juj dan Ma'juj, terbitnya matahari dari barat) sebagai realitas literal, dan hindari spekulasi tentang waktu atau urutan yang tepat di luar apa yang ditetapkan teks. Konservatisme interpretatif ini khas dari pendekatan metodologis al-Baghawi yang lebih luas.
content_ar: The opening sections of Sharh al-السنة address the foundations of Islamic الاعتقاد, beginning with the famous الحديث of جبريل ﷺ that defines the three dimensions of the religion: الإسلام (the outward acts of worship), الإيمان (الإيمان and its articles), and Ihsan (the dimension of spiritual excellence). Al-Baghawi's treatment of this foundational الحديث establishes the theological framework within which the rest of the work operates. The الحديث of جبريل ﷺ Al-Baghawi presents the الحديث of جبريل ﷺ — in which the Angel جبريل ﷺ appeared to النبي ﷺ in the form of a man and asked him about الإسلام, الإيمان, Ihsan, and the signs of the Last Hour — as the master text for الفهم the religion. النبي ﷺ's definitions in this الحديث: الإسلام as the five pillars (testimony, الصلاة, الزكاة, الصيام, الحج); الإيمان as الاعتقاد in الله, His الملائكة, His الكتب, His الرسل, the Last Day, and divine predetermination; and Ihsan as worshipping God as إذا you see Him, and إذا you do not see Him, knowing that He sees you — provide the organizing framework for al-Baghawi's entire work. He explains the relationship between الإسلام and الإيمان, addressing the العلمي debate about whether they are identical or whether الإيمان is a subset of الإسلام or vice versa. The Murjite position (that الإيمان is purely internal الاعتقاد, not بما في ذلك acts) and the Kharijite position (that الذنوب exclude one from الإيمان entirely) are both rejected in النعمة of the mainstream Sunni view: الإيمان includes both الاعتقاد and action, and increases and decreases with obedience and disobedience. The Articles of الإيمان Al-Baghawi presents the articles of Islamic الإيمان systematically, drawing on the hadiths that establish each one. الاعتقاد in divine predetermination ( القدر ) receives particularly careful treatment, as it was the subject of the most bitter theological controversies of the early Islamic period. He explains the mainstream Sunni position: God has decreed all things and nothing occurs without His will and العلم, but human beings have genuine choice and are responsible for their choices. This position charts a middle الطريق between pure determinism (which would deny human moral المسؤولية) and the Mutazilite position (which al-Baghawi reads as limiting divine foreknowledge and power). The Signs of the Last Hour The final part of al-Baghawi's treatment of the الحديث of جبريل ﷺ addresses the signs of the Last Hour — the events that will precede the يوم القيامة. He presents the minor signs (already occurring in النبي ﷺ's era and بعد) and the major signs (which will occur near the end of time). This الفصل draws on a rich الحديث التراث about eschatology that al-Baghawi presents with careful attention to the reliability of the individual hadiths and the various العلمي interpretations of their meaning.
